purpose: Normalize BacDive download options and full BacDive metadata into a future strain-selection catalog for G4Vista table rebuilds.
designPrinciples: Do not align this structure to legacy microbial_environment_g4 query tables.
Use BacDive full metadata as the source of truth for option-driven strain filtering.
Use bacdive_id as the primary strain-selection key, then join to G4Vista assemblies through resolved_accession.
Keep raw BacDive option names and metadata paths so table rebuild code can be deterministic and auditable.
Represent arrays as one value row per array item where possible, not as opaque JSON strings only.

g4vistaAssemblyBridgeSources.role: preferred BacDive strain to assembly bridge after NCBI accession resolution
g4vistaAssemblyBridgeSources.notes: Use resolved_accession as the canonical G4Vista assembly key when present.

g4vistaAssemblyBridgeSources.role: cleaned BacDive genome sequence metadata before accession resolution

g4vistaCoreJoinTargets.grain: one row per assembly_accession with assembly-level quadruplex sequence summary
one row per quadruplex sequence and gene relation category
one row per quadruplex sequence and feature relation category

recommendedFutureTables.grain: one row per bacdive_id and resolved assembly accession
one row per selectable BacDive metadata field
one row per bacdive_id, field_id, and normalized value item
recommendedFutureTables.notes: This generic table makes future option-driven strain filtering possible without creating one table per BacDive section.

recommendedFutureTables.grain: one row per option shown to users
materialized or cached result of a saved option-selection query

metadataFieldCatalog.label: medium used

metadataFieldCatalog.label: colony shape
enumeratedValues: circular convex with entire margins, rod - firm aggregation even during shaking

metadataFieldCatalog.label: further description
sampleValues: actinomycete with well-developed aerial mycelia that differentiate into long straight or flexuous spore chains, spores a

older colonies turn white and produce extensive aerial
hyphae
with mature aerial mycelium that develops into rod-shaped spore chains

metadataFieldCatalog.label: complex name
enumeratedValues: branched non-fragmented substrate mycelium with aerial hyphae

metadataFieldCatalog.label: further description
sampleValues: well-developed, branched substrate hyphae and aerial mycelium that differentiates into flexuous spore chains consisted o
aerial mycelia and curved spore-chains with 5-8 spherical spores (1.4 µm in diameter)
